bool operator==(const Area &lhs, const A.brief: Determines whether two Area objects are equal based on their vnum range boundaries.
bool operator==(const Area &lhs, const A.details: This inline operator== function compares two Area instances by checking their min_vnum and max_vnum attributes. These attributes define the vnum range of each area, which uniquely identifies an Area within the game world. The comparison is performed efficiently inline to facilitate quick equality checks, especially in algorithms and data structures that handle collections of Area objects. The function assumes that the vnum range is the primary identifier for an Area and does not consider other attributes such as state or internal components. This approach ensures that areas with identical vnum ranges are considered equal, reflecting their role in organizing game content based on vnum boundaries.

params.lhs: The first Area object to compare.
params.rhs: The second Area object to compare.

bool operator==(const Area &lhs, const A.returns: True if both Area objects have identical min_vnum and max_vnum values; otherwise, false.
bool operator==(const Area &lhs, const A.notes: The function relies solely on vnum range boundaries for equality, which is suitable when vnum ranges are unique identifiers for areas. It does not consider other attributes such as area state or internal data, making it a lightweight comparison focused on spatial boundaries.
bool operator==(const Area &lhs, const A.rationale: This operator is used when determining if two Area instances represent the same region within the game world, particularly in contexts where vnum ranges are the primary means of identification, such as in area management, loading, or validation routines.
bool operator==(const Area &lhs, const A.name: operator==

bool operator!=(const Area &lhs, const A.brief: Checks whether two Area objects are not equal by negating their equality comparison.
bool operator!=(const Area &lhs, const A.details: This inline operator!= function determines if two Area instances, lhs and rhs, are not equal by invoking the operator== to compare their internal states. It then negates the result to return true when the objects differ and false when they are identical. This implementation ensures consistency between equality and inequality operators and leverages the existing equality logic, which likely compares various internal properties such as regions, data, and state information within the Area objects. The function provides a simple and reliable way to perform inequality checks in code that manipulates game world regions.

params.lhs: The first Area object to compare.
params.rhs: The second Area object to compare.

bool operator!=(const Area &lhs, const A.returns: True if the two Area objects are not equal; false otherwise.
bool operator!=(const Area &lhs, const A.throws: No exceptions are thrown by this function.
bool operator!=(const Area &lhs, const A.notes: This operator relies on the correctness of the operator== implementation for the Area class, ensuring that inequality is accurately determined based on internal state comparisons.
bool operator!=(const Area &lhs, const A.rationale: This function is used when code needs to determine if two Area instances differ, such as in validation, change detection, or conditional logic involving game world regions.
bool operator!=(const Area &lhs, const A.name: operator!=
